channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Myanmar earthquake death toll rises to 3,145 as more bodies found
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from the earthquake that hit Myanmar nearly a week ago rose Thursday to 3,145 as search and rescue teams found more bodies, the military-led government said, and humanitarian aid groups scrambled to provide survivors medical care and shelter.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 9 people killed in southwestern Syria following countrywide Israeli strikes overnight
item.description: NAWA, Syria (AP) — Israeli strikes in Syria reportedly killed at least nine people in the southwest of the country on Thursday, as Israel accused Turkey of trying to build a “protectorate” in Syria.
item.dc:creator: MALEK KHATTAB and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Slovaks rally to protest against legislation that critics say is harmful to NGOs
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands marched through Slovakia's capital on Thursday before a planned rally in front of parliament to condemn draft legislation that critics consider detrimental for many nongovernmental organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans oppose deporting migrants to El Salvador without due process: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans oppose deporting migrants who have not been convicted to El Salvador without due process, according to a new survey, as the Trump administration defends its deportation efforts amid broad scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to give Congress more power over tariffs
item.description: Senators have introduced bipartisan legislation to grant Congress more power over instituting tariffs on other countries following President Trump’s announcement of wide-ranging taxes on nearly all U.S. foreign trading partners. The bill, co-sponsored by Sens. Maria Cantwell (D-Wash.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The White House defended not including Russia, North Korea, Cuba or Belarus in the latest round of tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump levies tariffs on uninhabited islands
item.description: President Trump, as part of his expansive package of new tariffs, levied taxes on a number of uninhabited or sparsely populated islands that have little-to-no exports. The announcement of new tariffs on Wednesday will hit nearly all foreign countries with a baseline tax at 10 percent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Rubio demands NATO pony up, dismisses 'hysteria' over US role under Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NATO allies they must make hard choices and increase defense spending to 5% to bolster capabilities in face of emboldened adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The delicate dance to preserve the magic of Abbey Road's legendary Studio One
item.description: Abbey Road's Studio One is where the scores of “Star Wars,” “Harry Potter,” and “Wicked” were recorded.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Fox, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Post-earthquake ceasefires in Myanmar offer rare opportunity for disaster diplomacy
item.description: Temporary ceasefires announced by warring groups in Myanmar in the wake of last week’s devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake represent a rare de-escalation of a conflict that since 2021 has taken thousands of lives and uprooted more than 3 million people.
item.dc:creator: Grant Peck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio arrives in Brussels for NATO talks amid unease over Trump’s agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived at in Brussels Thursday for talks that allies hope will shed light on the U.S.’ future plans in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio tries to reassure wary allies of US commitment to NATO as Trump sends mixed signals
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Trump administration’s new envoy to NATO are seeking to reassure wary NATO members of the U.S. commitment to the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: More than a dozen migrants die as boats sink off Greece and Turkey
item.description: Greek and Turkish officials have reported the sinking of two boats carrying migrants in the early hours of Thursday in the narrow stretch of sea between Turkey and a nearby Greek island, leaving a total of at least 16 people dead, including three children, one missing and more than 40 survivors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Markets plunge as Trump tariffs deliver shock waves to world economy
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe volley of tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump tariffs likely to spur recession: Moody's economist
item.description: Mark Zandi said the situation may not be as grave if countries do not respond “in kind” to the president’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's PM Carney vows to ‘fight’ Trump’s tariffs, other world leaders weigh impact
item.description: President Trump's newest tariffs sparked pledges by Canada's prime minster to 'fight' back while some said they were committed to working with partners and others look to take on Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweeping Trump tariffs shock global economy, drawing threats and calls for talks
item.description: Tariff hikes announced by U.S. President Donald Trump have provoked dismay, threats of countermeasures and calls for further negotiations to make trade rules fairer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leading Canadian conservative says Ottawa should remove all tariffs as 'Liberation Day' arrives
item.description: As President Donald Trump’s tariff plan nears, Canadian politician Maxime Bernier calls for scrapping all tariffs, saying Canada’s duties hurt its own people more than the U.S., and a new trade era should begin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Danish prime minister visiting Greenland amid Trump takeover talk
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen will visit Greenland this week after the Trump administration ramps up its rhetoric about a takeover of the semiautonomous Danish territory. Frederiksen will meet with the territory's new leader, Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump unveils 'Liberation Day' tariffs, raising trade tensions
item.description: The tariffs goals include reducing the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and boosting American-made manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Canada stares down consequences of Trump tariff war: Job losses, grocery price hikes, possible recession
item.description: Canadians are bracing alongside other geopolitical partners as President Donald Trump prepares to unleash his next round of tariffs in what he is calling "liberation day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli defense minister says military is expanding its operation in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s military operation in the Gaza Strip is expanding to seize “large areas,” the defense minister said Wednesday, as airstrikes on the southern city of Khan Younis killed 17 people overnight, according to hospital officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation
